label: Take a short walk

reasonWhen.lowUv: It is dry and today's peak UV is low.
reasonWhen.default: It is dry, daylight, and today's peak UV is moderate or lower.

research: WHO: all physical activity counts; nature-based walking can support wellbeing.

label: Try a 5-minute mindfulness pause

reasonWhen.withdrawalHeavy: More energy is going out than coming in, so a low-demand reset may fit.
reasonWhen.default: A brief, low-demand pause can be an easy way to add energy.

research: Brief mindfulness and acceptance-based practices may reduce immediate anxiety.

label: Take a 10-minute screen break

reasonWhen.default: A short break can reduce fatigue without asking much of the remaining capacity.

research: A meta-analysis found micro-breaks improved vigor and reduced fatigue.

label: Do a gentle stretch

reasonWhen.outdoorSafe: A light movement option is available if going outside feels too large.
reasonWhen.default: Conditions favor simple indoor movement that adds energy.

research: WHO recommends replacing sedentary time with movement of any intensity.

label: Read a short poem

reasonWhen.default: A few lines of poetry can add energy without asking much of the body.

research: Reading for pleasure is linked with wellbeing and lower reported stress.

label: Journal for five minutes

reasonWhen.withdrawalHeavy: Writing things down can settle a day that has used more than it has added.
reasonWhen.default: A short journal pass can clarify the day and add a little energy back.

research: Expressive writing can support emotional processing in brief sessions.

label: Write a few sentences to someone you care about

reasonWhen.default: A short note keeps connection alive without needing to leave your chair.

research: Supportive social contact is associated with better day-to-day wellbeing.

label: Call or text someone you care about

reasonWhen.withdrawalHeavy: A quick check-in with kith or kin can restore energy when the day feels depleting.
reasonWhen.default: A short call or text is a low-demand way to add connection energy.

research: Social connection is a protective factor for mental health across the life course.
